title: [Kconfig:145-148] CONFIG_LICHEN_IPV6_ULA_PREFIX string not validated for fd00::/8 format
description: Critic: Input validation gap. What: CONFIG_LICHEN_IPV6_ULA_PREFIX is an arbitrary string with default 'fd00:lich:en00::' - no compile-time or runtime format validation at Kconfig level. Why it matters: Malformed prefixes (wrong length, invalid chars, non-fd00::/8) cause runtime -EINVAL in lichen_make_ula() but silently compile. Users might set invalid values unaware. A Kconfig string cannot enforce prefix format, so lichen_make_ula() is the validator, but error is delayed until runtime. Consider: Add BUILD_ASSERT or early boot validation of configured prefix.
close_reason: Documented that runtime validation in lichen_make_ula() is the enforcement point for fd00::/8 format. Added note to Kconfig help text explaining Kconfig cannot validate string format at compile time.
